versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 14 So the LORD God said to the serpent:
cursed are you above all livestock
and every beast of the field!
On your belly will you go,
all the days of your life.
15 And I will put enmity between you and the woman,
and between your seed and her seed.
and you will strike his heel.”

context_lines: In **Genesis 3**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Genesis.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **The Fate of the Serpent** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Genesis 3** remains the first control on meaning.
current_position_lines: No defined position is stated here yet beyond the common interpretations above. As related study develops, this section will be updated where the text warrants a clearer conclusion.
